name: Synctera Banking-as-a-Service API
what_it_does: Synctera banking-as-a-service platform REST API for fintechs and brands to build embedded banking products including deposit accounts, payment cards, ACH transfers, and lending through Synctera's sponsor bank network. Enables AI agents to manage customer account opening and KYC onboarding for embedded banking customer automation, handle deposit account creation and balance management for fintech banking product automation, access debit card issuance and card program management for embedded card product automation, retrieve ACH and wire transfer execution for payment product automation, manage transaction categorization and enrichment for banking data intelligence automation, handle ledger and financial accounting for embedded banking financial operations automation, access real-time balance and account analytics for banking product monitoring automation, retrieve compliance and regulatory reporting for BaaS program automation, manage sponsor bank relationship and program configuration for banking program management automation, and integrate Synctera with identity verification, fraud, and compliance platforms for end-to-end embedded banking product development.
use_cases: Opening customer accounts from embedded banking automation agents
Issuing debit cards from fintech card program agents
Processing ACH payments from embedded payment automation agents
Managing banking compliance from BaaS program agents
not_for: Enterprise corporate treasury and cash management (use major bank treasury APIs)
Crypto exchange and digital asset banking (use Paxos or Silvergate)
Mortgage and complex lending origination (use Encompass or ICE Mortgage)
best_when: A fintech startup or brand wanting AI agents to automate embedded banking product development including deposit accounts, debit cards, ACH payments, and KYC onboarding using Synctera's sponsor bank-connected BaaS platform.
avoid_when: FDIC DEPOSIT INSURANCE PASS-THROUGH REQUIREMENTS: Embedded banking deposit accounts via Synctera must comply with FDIC pass-through insurance disclosure requirements; automated account opening without FDIC pass-through insurance disclosure creates regulatory non-compliance for fintech deposit account products. OCC BANK CHARTER THIRD-PARTY RISK: Synctera's sponsor bank partners have OCC/FDIC third-party risk management obligations; Synctera fintechs are subject to sponsor bank oversight and examination; automated banking product operations must comply with sponsor bank policies that may change during OCC examination cycles. REGULATION E DISPUTE AND ERROR RESOLUTION: Automated payment and transfer operations via Synctera create Regulation E obligation for timely dispute resolution (10 business days investigation, 45 days provisional credit); automated payment systems must implement Regulation E dispute workflow; automated payment processing without dispute management creates Regulation E violation for consumer fintech accounts.

auth.notes: Synctera uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2020 by Peter McLaughlin. Private (~$58M raised, Lightspeed, Fin Capital). Banking-as-a-service platform connecting fintechs to sponsor banks. Sponsor bank network: Lineage Bank, Pacific Western Bank, others. Deposit accounts, debit cards, ACH, wires, and lending products. KYC/KYB onboarding. Open-source SDK examples. Competes with Unit, Treasury Prime, and Stripe Treasury for BaaS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — usage-based subscription with platform fee
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly platform fee plus per-transaction fees; sandbox environment for development; production requires sponsor bank approval
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. Private (~$58M raised). Platform fee plus per-transaction. Sandbox available. Sponsor bank approval required. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; account and transaction queries throttled; webhooks for account, transaction, and compliance events

known_agent_gotchas: SPONSOR BANK APPROVAL REQUIRED BEFORE PRODUCTION: Synctera production access requires sponsor bank program approval; automated fintech product development in sandbox cannot launch without sponsor bank underwriting and approval; automated production launch timeline must include sponsor bank review and approval period (weeks to months depending on product complexity)

IDEMPOTENCY KEY REQUIRED FOR ALL PAYMENT OPERATIONS: Synctera payment API supports idempotency keys for ACH and wire transfers; automated payment operations must implement idempotency keys; duplicate payment submission without idempotency key creates double payments that require manual reversal coordination with sponsor bank

ACH RETURN HANDLING FOR AUTOMATED PAYMENT FLOWS: ACH returns from Synctera can arrive 2-5 business days after payment submission; automated payment confirmation workflows must handle late ACH returns; automated settlement that treats ACH as final before return window closes creates premature settlement for payments that subsequently return

CARD TRANSACTION AUTHORIZATION vs SETTLEMENT TIMING: Synctera card transactions have authorization hold followed by settlement (1-3 days); automated balance calculations using authorized amount must account for settled vs authorized amounts; automated account balance reporting without settled/authorized amount distinction creates customer-facing balance inaccuracy

REGULATORY CHANGE VELOCITY FOR BAAS PRODUCTS: BaaS regulatory environment is rapidly evolving (FDIC guidance on fintech deposits, OCC bank-fintech partnership policies); automated product configuration may require changes in response to regulatory guidance updates; monitor BaaS regulatory developments and Synctera program policy updates for required automated workflow changes
agent_readiness.security_notes: Banking-as-a-service. SOC2, FDIC-insured, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. US. Deposit accounts, payment transactions, and KYC/KYB data.

security_score_components.security_notes: BaaS. SOC2, FDIC-insured, PCI-DSS. OAuth2. US. Deposit accounts, payments, and KYC data.
